zh.lifeSummary: 當Alicia Maria de Verdon於1212年出生在英國諾福爾可布雷辛加姆時，她的父親William De Verdon37歲，母親Mrs Maud De William39歲。她於1232年與Mathew de Hulgreve在英國爾邱奇 明書爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭爾希布盧可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alicia Maria de Verdon于1212年出生在英国諾福爾可布雷辛加姆时，她的父亲William De Verdon37岁，母亲Mrs Maud De William39岁。她于1232年与Mathew de Hulgreve在英国爾邱奇 明書爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰爾希布盧可。

nameMeanings.name: Verdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Ramon, Angel, Luis, Ruben, Azael, Fernando, Fidel, Francisco, Guillermo, Javier.
English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of   Verdon  .
Altered form of French   Verdun  .

variants.name: Ferdon
meanings: Altered form, under American Dutch influence in New Netherland, of French   Verdon  .

variants.name: Wearden
meanings: English (Lancashire): apparently from a placename with Old English denu ‘valley’ as the second element. However, the Victoria County History for Staffordshire identifies John de Werdon, mentioned in 1280, as having his name from Verdun (Manche, France); see Verdon .

variants.name: Verdun
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Alicia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisha
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisia
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alyssa
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alissa .

variants.name: Alissa
meanings: Contracted form of   Alicia .

variants.name: Alesha
meanings: Respelling of   Alicia .

nameMeanings.name: Maria
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Alicia Maria de Verdon was born in 1212, in Bressingham, Norfolk, England, her father, William De Verdon, was 37 and her mother, Mrs Maud De William, was 39. She married Mathew de Hulgreve in 1232, in Church Minshull, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in Shipbrook, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alicia Maria de Verdon est née en 1212, à Bressingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, William De Verdon, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Mrs Maud De William, avait 39 ans. Elle a épousé Mathew de Hulgreve en 1232, à Church Minshull, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée à Shipbrook, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alicia Maria de Verdon 1212 in Bressingham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William De Verdon 37 und ihre Mutter Mrs Maud De William 39. Sie hat 1232 in Church Minshull, Cheshire, England Mathew de Hulgreve geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb in Shipbrook, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alicia Maria de Verdon è nata il nel 1212 nel Bressingham, Norfolk, England, sua padre, William De Verdon, aveva 37 anni e sua madre, Mrs Maud De William, aveva 39 anni. Lei sposato Mathew de Hulgreve nel 1232, nel Church Minshull, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel Shipbrook, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Alicia Maria de Verdonが1212年にイギリスノーフォルクブレシンガムで生まれた時，父William De Verdonは37歳，母Mrs Maud De Williamは39歳であった。1232年にイギリスチェシュアイアチャーチ ミンシュルでMathew de Hulgreveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアシュイプブルークで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alicia Maria de Verdon(이)가 1212년 영국 노르폴크 브레징함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William De Verdon(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs Maud De William(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 1232년 영국 체실르 c후르치 민슈에서 Mathew de Hulgreve(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 시브룩에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alicia Maria de Verdon nasceu em 1212, em Bressingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William De Verdon, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Maud De William, tinha 39 anos. Ela casou-se com Mathew de Hulgreve em 1232, em Church Minshull, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em Shipbrook, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alicia Maria de Verdon родилась в 1212 г. в местности брессингхам, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William De Verdon было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Maud De William, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Mathew de Hulgreve в 1232 г. в местности чурч миншулл, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в местности шипбрук, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alicia Maria de Verdon nació en 1212, en Bressingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William De Verdon, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Mrs Maud De William, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Mathew de Hulgreve en 1232, en Church Minshull, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en Shipbrook, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
